title: Assertion failure on finalization with `_lsprof` and `asyncio` in 3.12
author: devdanzin
body:
# Crash report

### What happened?

It's possible to abort the interpreter in 3.12 during finalization by having a mix of enabled `_lsprof` and `asyncio` with very specific details:

```python
import asyncio
import _lsprof

obj = _lsprof.Profiler()
obj.enable()
obj._pystart_callback(lambda: 0, 0)
obj = None  # Required

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
```
Not having `loop` stored in a variable or not reassigning `obj` breaks the testcase.
Abort message:
```
python: Python/instrumentation.c:958: call_instrumentation_vector: Assertion `instrumentation_cross_checks(tstate->interp, code)' failed.
Aborted
```

Found using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner.

### CPython versions tested on:

3.12

### Operating systems tested on:

Linux

### Output from running 'python -VV' on the command line:

Python 3.12.8+ (heads/3.12:b0615a8a9aa, Dec 11 2024, 13:46:42) [GCC 11.4.0]

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: It fails with the same message.

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body: The reproducer seems to pass just fine on release builds. I suspect that the assertion just needs to get removed.

comments.author: gaogaotiantian
comments.body: No please do not just remove random assertions. That assertion indicates that on that specific code object, the monitoring events are not consistent. We should figure out why.

comments.author: gaogaotiantian
comments.body: Hmm, it seems to be fine with main branch as well. @devdanzin does it only repro on 3.12?

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: Yes, I was only able to hit the assertion failure in 3.12.

comments.author: gaogaotiantian
comments.body: Hmm, if it's fixed somehow in 3.13, and it's a very rare issue (only the assertion failure, no observable problem with released branch), it's probably not worth it to investigate it. (It's pretty close to the last bug-fix release for 3.12 too).

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body: Should we just close this, then?

comments.author: gaogaotiantian
comments.body: I'm inclined to close this, unless others disagree.

comments.author: gaogaotiantian
comments.body: Let's close this for now. If anyone would like to keep it open feel free to reopen it. Just remember that that last bug-fix version 3.12.10 is scheduled to be released on 4/8/2025. If we can't make that deadline, the fix won't be merged.
